news.title: Sedimentary sleuths
news.summary_en: K-State biological and agricultural engineer Trisha Moore is leading a project to test the effect of water injection dredging on sediment levels in the Big Blue and Kansas Rivers.

news.title: A destination for rodeo and more
news.summary_en: Built for rodeo and designed for more, the Bilbrey Family Event Center is creating a new gathering place for K-State and Kansas agriculture.

news.title: Healthcare from classroom to community
news.summary_en: Through the Applied Behavioral Health Counseling course, students apply classroom concepts during health coaching sessions with real clients from the community.

news.title: Digging deeper
news.summary_en: The Kirmser Undergraduate Research Award honors undergraduate students at Kansas State University whose research is strengthened by the resources and support of K-State Libraries.

news.title: A study garden to love full well
news.summary_en: Kansas State University student Katie Chandler turned a competition-winning concept into a major outdoor campus improvement near Hale Library.

news.title: K-State Blue Key Senior Honorary initiates 2026-2027 class
news.summary_en: The Kansas State University chapter of Blue Key Senior Honorary, or Blue Key, has initiated 16 new members for the 2026-2027 academic year.

news.title: Nordic visions, urban futures

news.title: Computer science department to premiere AI and data science children’s film at local theater
news.summary_en: The Department of Computer Science will screen "A Data Science Tale: When Data and Pixel Met" — a movie aimed at educating children ages 4-8 about the basic concepts behind artificial...

news.title: Enjoy flexible summer campus parking
news.summary_en: With summer open parking, you can use most lots across campus, regardless of your permit type, beginning May 18. However, please remember that a valid permit is still required.
